source_pr.title: executor: fix issue of foreign key cascade don't work cause by untouched index key
source_pr.body:
Signed-off-by: crazycs520 <crazycs520@gmail.com>

<!--

Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?
<!--

Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and 
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/contribute-code.html#referring-to-an-issue.

-->

Issue Number: close #39419

Problem Summary:  Fix issue of foreign key cascade don't work cause by untouched index key

### What is changed and how it works?

### Check List

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [ ] Unit test
- [x] Integration test
- [ ] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)
- [ ] No code

Side effects

- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU
- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory
- [ ] Breaking backward compatibility

Documentation

- [ ] Affects user behaviors
- [ ] Contains syntax changes
- [ ] Contains variable changes
- [ ] Contains experimental features
- [ ] Changes MySQL compatibility

### Release note

<!-- compatibility change, improvement, bugfix, and new feature need a release note -->

Please refer to [Release Notes Language Style Guide](https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/release-notes-style-guide.html) to write a quality release note.

```release-note
None
```

symptoms: Foreign key cascade can observe data-index inconsistency when autocommit updates do not rebuild untouched index keys; related FK tests may fail.
failure_signature: No explicit flaky failure signature in patch (foreign key cascade correctness fix).
root_cause_explanation: INSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE: The patch is a correctness fix for foreign key cascade handling (ensure indices are rebuilt/written when FK cascade is pending, even if the index key is otherwise considered untouched under autocommit), plus regression tests. The PR/case does not include a concrete intermittent flaky failure signature, so keep this case as needs-more-evidence rather than guessing a flakiness category.
fix_pattern: Rebuild/write index keys during FK cascade to avoid data-index inconsistency under autocommit.

evidence: evidence: tk.MustQuery("select * from t1 order by id").Check(testkit.Rows("2", "11", "13")) (executor/fktest/foreign_key_test.go)

analysis:

PR url: https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/pull/39420

Triage summary: tk.MustQuery("select * from t1 order by id").Check(testkit.Rows("2", "11", "13")) (executor/fktest/foreign_key_test.go)
Changed files (2): executor/fktest/foreign_key_test.go, table/tables/tables.go
Patch url: https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/pull/39420.patch
Picked test: type=unit path=executor/fktest/foreign_key_test.go name=foreign_key_test
